title: podman build --squash always rebuilds every layer
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

So I'm having a problem were the behaviour of the `--squash` flag is very different between `docker build` and `podman build`

With `docker build --squash`, when I try to build the same Dockerfile multiple times in sequence (without any changes to it, nor any file), the layers are only actually built the first time, while the following builds only reuse the cached layers and re-squash them at the end, resulting in a new image hash, but with a drastically smaller build time, since the layers were used from cache.

With `podman build --squash`, the behaviour is different. When I take the same Dockerfile and try to build it multiple times in sequence (again, no changes whatsoever), all the layers are rebuilt everytime, on top of the expected squash at the end, as if I had passed the `--no-cache` flag as well.

I'm not sure if this difference is expected, but if so, I would kindly like to ask the reasoning, because it seems like the current behaviour with podman could easily be replicated with `--no-cache --squash`, without having to force the rebuild of all layers when using only `--squash`


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. In my testing, any Dockerfile seems to reproduce this issue, but I'll leave a really simple one I'm using:
```
FROM fedora:36
WORKDIR /app

RUN dnf update -y

RUN dnf install -y \
python3-pip \
libpq-devel

RUN dnf clean all
```

2. Using any environment with docker, call this command twice in a row:
```bash
docker build --squash -f Dockerfile . -t myimage
```
The second execution should be significantly faster, using the cached layers, and resulting in an output roughly like this:
```bash
Sending build context to Docker daemon  1.674MB
Step 1/5 : FROM fedora:36
 ---> 98ffdbffd207
Step 2/5 : WORKDIR /app
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 6f3050febb4c
Step 3/5 : RUN dnf update -y
 ---> Using cache
 ---> eb0c3421bcb3
Step 4/5 : RUN dnf install -y python3-pip libpq-devel
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 6a82d35bc559
Step 5/5 : RUN dnf clean all
 ---> Using cache
 ---> a1c80f141586
Successfully built bb03750eaeda
Successfully tagged myimage:latest
```

3. Now in an envinronment using podman, do the same step of calling the build twice in a row, and the build process should be roughly the same duration both times, with the output of a full build both times:
```bash
podman build --squash -f Dockerfile . -t myimage
```


**Describe the results you received:**

Using the `--squash` results in the cached layers never being used, causing high times on rebuilds that could be avoided.


**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected the `--quash` to work the same as docker's, with the cached layers still being used, but with the squash at the end


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7
Built:      Tue Mar 15 19:15:06 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

- Docker: Ubuntu 22.04
- Podman: CentOS Stream 8

comments.body:
Hi @lucasbaile , Thanks for creating the issue.

In order to use layer caching with squash, please use `--layers --squash-all` in podman. This feature was added a while ago https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14320 so something like `podman build --layers --squash-all -f Dockerfile . -t myimage` should be enough for your use-case.

Please try the above command and let me know if you face any issues. :smile: 

Thanks

Hey, @flouthoc!

Thanks for replying so fast!

I'm not sure if I'm doing something wrong or if the mentioned feature is not present on my 4.0.2 podman, but I'm getting this error when running that command:

```
[root@centos ~]# podman build --layers --squash-all -f Dockerfile . -t myimage
Error: cannot specify --squash, --squash-all and --layers options together
```
Your podman seems old to me this patch was recently merged. Could you build source from upstream ?

Yeah, I noticed that for some reason the podman rpm packages stopped being released at 4.0.2 for CentOS Stream 8 (el8), and I'm currently locked to this OS because of some clients servers.

I'll test using the latest version from source and will update there after, thanks!

Hi @lucasbaile , I checked this again this is working in upstream and we already have a test for this added here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14320 . 

I am closing this since this should not persist in upstream but please comment back here if this does not works for you and I'll re-open the issue :smile: 

Thanks
